answer:

---

# Trump Cabinet Departures & Tulsi Gabbard DNI Status — Research Report (as of April 14, 2026)

## 1. Complete List of Major Departures from Trump's Second Term

### Pre-Inauguration Withdrawal
- **Matt Gaetz** — Nominated for Attorney General (Nov 2024), withdrew before taking office amid bipartisan Senate resistance over allegations of paying underage women for sexual encounters. Resigned from Congress; seat filled by Jimmy Patronis.

### 2025 Departures

| Official | Position | Departure Date | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Vivek Ramaswamy** | Co-head of DOGE | Early 2025 | Left to pursue elected office; position not formally filled |
| **Elon Musk** | Co-head of DOGE | 2025 | Left post (details unclear) |
| **Douglas O'Donnell** | Acting IRS Commissioner | Late Feb 2025 | Retired |
| **Melanie Krause** | Acting IRS Commissioner | Mid-April 2025 | Resigned after administration agreed to share taxpayer data with immigration authorities |
| **Gary Shapley** | Acting IRS Commissioner | Mid-April 2025 | Removed days after appointment; Treasury Sec. Bessent complained he wasn't consulted |
| **Cameron Hamilton** | Acting FEMA Administrator | May 2025 | Fired after testifying to Congress that he opposed dismantling FEMA |
| **Mike Waltz** | National Security Adviser | May 2025 | Removed after Signal group chat scandal re: Yemen military operations; reassigned as UN Ambassador (confirmed Sept 2025) |
| **Susan Monarez** | CDC Director | Late Aug 2025 | Fired weeks after confirmation; White House said she wasn't "aligned with the President's agenda"; she said she refused to "rubber-stamp unscientific, reckless directive" |
| **Billy Long** | IRS Commissioner | Aug 2025 | Removed ~2 months after confirmation; clashes with Treasury Department. Nominated as Ambassador to Iceland |
| **Lindsey Halligan** | U.S. Attorney, E.D. Virginia | Sept 2025–Jan 2026 | A federal judge ruled she was unlawfully appointed; stepped down, left DOJ |
| **Dan Bongino** | Deputy Director, FBI | 2025 (exact date unclear) | Stepped down to return to media career |

### 2026 Departures

| Official | Position | Departure Date | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Kristi Noem** | Secretary of Homeland Security | March 5, 2026 | Fired. Controversy over congressional testimony blowups, $220M self-promotional ad campaign, fallout from fatal shootings by federal immigration officers in Minneapolis (Jan 2026). Replaced by **Markwayne Mullin** (confirmed March 2026) |
| **Joe Kent** | Director, National Counterterrorism Center | ~March 17, 2026 | Resigned over Iran war, stating Iran posed "no imminent threat" and accusing Israel of misleading Trump |
| **Pam Bondi** | Attorney General | April 2, 2026 | Fired. Trump frustrated with slow pace of prosecuting political adversaries, handling of Epstein files. Replaced by **Todd Blanche** (acting); **Lee Zeldin** being considered for permanent role |
| **Gen. Randy George** | Army Chief of Staff | April 3, 2026 | Forced into immediate retirement by Defense Sec. Hegseth |
| **Gen. David Hodne** | Commanding General, Army Transformation & Training | April 3, 2026 | Retired alongside Gen. George |
| **Maj. Gen. William Green Jr.** | Army Chief of Chaplains | April 3, 2026 | Retired alongside Gen. George |

### Officials Reportedly At Risk (as of mid-April 2026)
- **Howard Lutnick** (Commerce Secretary) — "on thin ice"; connected to Epstein files; fallen out of favor with Chief of Staff Susie Wiles
- **Lori Chavez-DeRemer** (Labor Secretary) — husband banned from DOL headquarters after sexual assault allegations against female staff; two top aides resigned amid misconduct investigation; allegations of drinking in office
- **Kash Patel** (FBI Director) — reportedly under discussion for removal; dealing with Iranian-backed hacker attack releasing 10 years of personal emails
- **Dan Driscoll** (Army Secretary) — rift with Defense Secretary Hegseth
- **Tulsi Gabbard** (DNI) — nearly fired; retained after Roger Stone intervention

---

## 2. Tulsi Gabbard — DNI Status (Deep Dive)

### Current Status: STILL IN OFFICE as of April 14, 2026
Gabbard was confirmed as the 8th Director of National Intelligence and has held the position since approximately February 2025. She oversees 18 U.S. intelligence agencies.

### Key Timeline of Events

- **January 2025**: Confirmed as DNI
- **July 2025**: Ordered declassification of "Russia-gate" documents; oversaw intelligence support for "Operation Midnight Hammer" (strikes on Iran's nuclear program)
- **March 17, 2026**: Her deputy **Joe Kent** resigned from the National Counterterrorism Center, publicly criticizing the Iran war
- **March 18, 2026**: Gabbard testified before Senate Intelligence Committee, presenting the 2026 Annual Threat Assessment. She controversially **omitted** a written paragraph stating Iran's enrichment capabilities were destroyed — drawing Democratic criticism. She said she "ran out of time."
- **March 18–20, 2026**: Rumors spread that she was preparing to resign; Sen. Tom Cotton led effort to strip DNI of authority
- **March 30, 2026**: Trump publicly stated he still had "confidence" in Gabbard, acknowledging she was "a little bit different in her thought process"
- **Early April 2026**: Trump **appeared ready to fire Gabbard**, polling Cabinet members about replacing her
- **~April 7–9, 2026**: **Roger Stone intervened** with a phone call, persuading Trump not to fire her. Stone argued it would: (1) create a damaging media cycle, (2) make her a martyr among anti-interventionist Republicans, (3) position her as a 2028 GOP presidential contender, (4) complicate JD Vance's 2028 path
- **April 6, 2026**: Unverified social media claims that Gabbard had been fired went viral (200,000+ views). White House communications director Steven Cheung called this "totally fake news" and said Trump retains "total confidence" in Gabbard
- **April 13, 2026**: Gabbard declassified documents related to Trump's 2019 impeachment, alleging intelligence community conspiracy
- **April 14, 2026**: Conservative commentary (HotAir) praised Gabbard's performance, noting she is "doing a great job" with declassification efforts

### Gabbard's Position — Key Tensions
- **Anti-interventionist stance**: Gabbard's longstanding opposition to "regime change" wars puts her at odds with the administration's Iran military operations
- **Refusal to condemn Joe Kent**: Trump was reportedly frustrated that she would not publicly criticize her departing deputy
- **Omission during testimony**: Her failure to read a key paragraph about Iran's nuclear capabilities was seen as either incompetence or deliberate distancing from the administration's war narrative
- **Support from MAGA base**: Roger Stone and others argue she is essential for "deep state reform" and declassification work, making her firing politically costly

### Prediction Market Data (Kalshi, as of April 14, 2026)

**"Who will leave Trump's Cabinet next?" (after April 2, 2026)**

| Official | Probability | Volume |
|---|---|---|
| Lori Chavez-DeRemer | **26%** | $57,514 |
| Pete Hegseth | **21%** | $101,655 |
| **Tulsi Gabbard** | **15%** | $87,586 |
| Howard Lutnick | **13%** | $60,691 |
| Susie Wiles | **6.9%** | $34,137 |
| Marco Rubio | **2.7%** | $16,069 |
| RFK Jr. | **2.7%** | $21,709 |
| All others | <2% each | — |

A separate report from early April cited a Kalshi market giving Gabbard a **78% probability of remaining in office through year-end 2026**.

### Brookings Turnover Data
- As of January 20, 2026, Trump's "A Team" turnover stood at **29%** — before the Noem and Bondi firings in March–April 2026.

---

## 3. Summary of Key Findings

- **Two Cabinet secretaries have been fired** in Trump's second term: Kristi Noem (DHS, March 5, 2026) and Pam Bondi (AG, April 2, 2026).
- **Multiple sub-cabinet officials** have been fired or resigned, including the NSA (Waltz), CDC Director (Monarez), FEMA Acting Administrator (Hamilton), multiple IRS commissioners, and the National Counterterrorism Center Director (Kent).
- **Tulsi Gabbard remains DNI** as of April 14, 2026, but came very close to being fired in early April. She was saved by Roger Stone's direct intervention with Trump.
- **Prediction markets** give Gabbard a 15% chance of being the *next* Cabinet member to leave, making her the third most likely after Chavez-DeRemer (26%) and Hegseth (21%).
- The White House has publicly reaffirmed "total confidence" in Gabbard multiple times, most recently after viral false firing rumors on April 6.
- Gabbard continues active work on declassification and testified before the Senate on March 18, 2026.

key_facts: Two Cabinet secretaries have been fired in Trump's second term: Kristi Noem (DHS, March 5, 2026) and Pam Bondi (AG, April 2, 2026).
Tulsi Gabbard remains Director of National Intelligence as of April 14, 2026, despite nearly being fired in early April 2026.
Roger Stone intervened with a phone call to Trump in early April 2026 to prevent Gabbard's firing, arguing it would make her a martyr and complicate JD Vance's 2028 path.
White House communications director Steven Cheung called firing rumors 'totally fake news' and said Trump retains 'total confidence' in Gabbard (April 6, 2026).
Gabbard's deputy Joe Kent resigned as Director of the National Counterterrorism Center around March 17, 2026, over opposition to the Iran war.
Gabbard testified before the Senate Intelligence Committee on March 18, 2026, controversially omitting a written paragraph about Iran's destroyed nuclear enrichment capabilities.
Other major departures include: Mike Waltz (NSA, May 2025), Cameron Hamilton (FEMA, May 2025), Susan Monarez (CDC, Aug 2025), Billy Long (IRS, Aug 2025).
Matt Gaetz withdrew as AG nominee before inauguration; Vivek Ramaswamy and Elon Musk left DOGE in early 2025.
Three Army generals were forced into retirement on April 3, 2026: Gen. Randy George (Chief of Staff), Gen. David Hodne, and Maj. Gen. William Green Jr.
Officials reportedly at risk of removal include: Lori Chavez-DeRemer (Labor), Pete Hegseth (Defense), Howard Lutnick (Commerce), Kash Patel (FBI), and Dan Driscoll (Army Secretary).
Kalshi prediction markets give Gabbard a 15% probability of being the next Cabinet member to leave, behind Chavez-DeRemer (26%) and Hegseth (21%).
A separate Kalshi market cited in early April gave Gabbard a 78% probability of remaining in office through the end of 2026.
Brookings reported Trump's 'A Team' turnover at 29% as of January 20, 2026, before the Noem and Bondi firings.
Gabbard declassified documents on April 13, 2026 alleging intelligence community conspiracy behind Trump's 2019 impeachment.
Gabbard presented the 2026 Annual Threat Assessment identifying China, Russia, Iran, North Korea, and Pakistan as top nuclear threats.
